source_docs.title: Single-cell analysis of genome and transcriptome diversity in humans

oligos.name: Adaptor-1x (Ad-1x) barcoded RT primer
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription primer: barcoded poly-T primer with a 5' Illumina adaptor and a T7 promoter overhang; primes first-strand cDNA from mRNA poly(A) tails (CEL-Seq primer, ref 13)

components.name: 5' Illumina adaptor
components.role: 5' Illumina adaptor overhang (exact sequence not printed in document; per CEL-Seq ref 13)

components.name: T7 promoter
components.role: T7 RNA polymerase promoter enabling in vitro transcription of cDNA into aRNA

components.name: Cell barcode
components.role: Cell-specific barcode identifying the cell of origin (length not stated in document)

components.name: Poly(dT)
components.role: Poly-T that anneals to mRNA poly(A) tail

oligos.notes: Exact nucleotide sequence not provided in the DR-Seq documents; described structurally per the referenced CEL-Seq primer (Hashimshony et al., Cell Reports 2012, ref 13). DR-Seq uses length-based identifiers instead of a random UMI.

oligos.name: Adaptor-2 (Ad-2) quasilinear amplification primer
oligos.role: MALBAC-style random-priming amplification primer: 27-nt defined 5' sequence followed by 8 random nucleotides; randomly primes gDNA and single-stranded cDNA during quasilinear whole-genome amplification

components.name: Ad-2 common 27-nt sequence
components.role: Defined 27-nt 5' common sequence (identical to primer P2; MALBAC common sequence, ref 7)

components.name: Random priming octamer
components.role: 8 random nucleotides for random priming; 3' end preferentially ends in GGG or TTT

oligos.notes: Paper states 'defined 27-nt sequence at the 5' end followed by eight random nucleotides (Ad-2)'; the 27-nt segment matches primer P2 (GTGAGTGATGGTTGAGGTAGTGTGGAG). Supplementary Note: 3' end has sequence GGG or TTT.

oligos.name: P1 second-strand synthesis primer
oligos.role: Primes mRNA-specific second-strand synthesis of the quasilinear-amplified cDNA (single PCR cycle) to regenerate the T7 promoter for IVT

oligos.name: P2 gDNA amplification primer
oligos.role: PCR primer that amplifies the gDNA-half quasilinear amplification product (21 cycles); anneals to the Ad-2 common sequence
oligos.notes: 5'-GTGAGTGATGGTTGAGGTAGTGTGGAG-3', 27 nt; identical to the Ad-2 common region.

oligos.name: P3 biotinylated adaptor-removal primer
oligos.role: 5'-biotinylated PCR primer used to replace/remove adaptor Ad-2 from the gDNA PCR product before Illumina library prep; biotinylated ends are captured on streptavidin beads and discarded

oligos.name: 3' Illumina adaptor (aRNA ligation)
oligos.role: 3' Illumina adaptor ligated to the IVT-generated aRNA in the mRNA branch prior to RT and PCR (CEL-Seq library prep, ref 13)
oligos.notes: Sequence not printed in the DR-Seq documents; library preparation performed as in CEL-Seq (ref 13), which uses Illumina TruSeq small-RNA adaptors.

oligos.name: Illumina index / library primers
oligos.role: Cell-specific index primers introducing sample indices and Illumina P5/P7 flanks during final library preparation (NEBNext Ultra DNA Library Prep Kit for the gDNA library; CEL-Seq index primers for the mRNA library)
oligos.notes: Exact index/adaptor sequences not printed in the DR-Seq documents.

oligos.name: Illumina P5 adapter
oligos.role: 5' Illumina flow-cell adapter added during Illumina library preparation
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina P5; introduced during Illumina library prep (not explicitly sequenced in the DR-Seq documents).

oligos.name: Illumina P7 adapter
oligos.role: 3' Illumina flow-cell adapter added during Illumina library preparation
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina P7 (final-library bottom-strand end is revcomp ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG); introduced during Illumina library prep (not explicitly sequenced in the DR-Seq documents).

final_library.source_label: DR-Seq mRNA (transcriptome) Illumina library — CEL-Seq-style; exact internal adaptor/barcode sequences are deferred to CEL-Seq (ref 13) and not printed in these documents
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC + [Illumina Read 1 adaptor] + [CELL_BARCODE] + [CDNA] + [3' Illumina / Read 2 adaptor] + [SAMPLE_INDEX] + ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG
annotation_lines: [Illumina Read 1 adaptor] = TruSeq Read 1 primer region (sequence per CEL-Seq ref 13; not printed)
[CELL_BARCODE] = Cell barcode (from Ad-1x; length not stated in document)
[CDNA] = cDNA insert (3' end of transcript; length-based identifier derived from Ad-2 priming position, no random UMI)
[3' Illumina / Read 2 adaptor] = TruSeq Read 2 adaptor region (ligated 3' Illumina adaptor; sequence not printed)

evidence.locator: DR-Seq mRNA (transcriptome) Illumina library — CEL-Seq-style; exact internal adaptor/barcode sequences are deferred to CEL-Seq (ref 13) and not printed in these documents

library_generation.title: Single-cell lysis & reverse transcription with barcoded Ad-1x
library_generation.summary: A hand-picked single cell is lysed and its mRNA reverse-transcribed with the barcoded poly-T primer Ad-1x, leaving intact gDNA alongside single-stranded cDNA.
library_generation.note: Arrayscript reverse transcriptase extends from the Ad-1x poly(T) annealed to mRNA poly(A); the cDNA acquires a 5' cell barcode, a 5' Illumina adaptor and a T7 promoter overhang. ERCC spike-ins are added at lysis.
library_generation.product:
gDNA (unmodified double-stranded genome):
5'- ...genomic DNA... -3'
3'- ...genomic DNA... -5'

First-strand cDNA (from mRNA):
5'- [ILLUMINA_ADAPTOR][T7_PROMOTER][CELL_BARCODE](T)n[CDNA] -3'
                                             3'- (A)n...mRNA...cap -5'

library_generation.title: Quasilinear whole-genome amplification with Ad-2 (7 rounds)
library_generation.summary: gDNA and single-stranded cDNA are co-amplified by 7 cycles of MALBAC-style quasilinear amplification using the random-priming adaptor Ad-2.
library_generation.note: Bst large fragment + Pyrophage 3173 exo- randomly prime both templates with Ad-2 (27-nt common + 8N). Most short (0.5-2.5 kb) amplicons carry Ad-2 at both ends; a minority of cDNA-derived amplicons carry Ad-2 at one end and Ad-1x at the other. The Ad-2 priming position gives each cDNA molecule a length-based identifier.
library_generation.product:
Majority amplicon (gDNA- or cDNA-derived, Ad-2 both ends):
5'- GTGAGTGATGGTTGAGGTAGTGTGGAGNNN[insert]NNNCTCCACACTACCTCAACCATCACTCAC -3'

Minority cDNA-derived amplicon (Ad-2 one end, Ad-1x other):
5'- GTGAGTGATGGTTGAGGTAGTGTGGAGNNN[CDNA](T)n[CELL_BARCODE][T7_PROMOTER][ILLUMINA_ADAPTOR] -3'

library_generation.title: Split sample into gDNA and mRNA halves
library_generation.summary: After 7 rounds of quasilinear amplification the reaction is divided in two, processed separately for gDNA and mRNA sequencing.
library_generation.note: No physical separation of nucleic acids occurred before amplification; the split simply routes half toward genomic-DNA library prep and half toward transcriptome (IVT) library prep.
library_generation.product:
Half A -> gDNA branch (steps 4-7)
Half B -> mRNA branch (steps 8-11)

library_generation.title: gDNA branch: PCR amplification with P2 (21 cycles)
library_generation.summary: The gDNA half is PCR-amplified for 21 cycles with primer P2 against the Ad-2 common sequence.
library_generation.note: Deep VentR (exo-) exponentially amplifies the Ad-2-flanked amplicons.

library_generation.title: gDNA branch: Ad-2 removal with biotinylated P3 PCR
library_generation.summary: A short PCR with the 5'-biotinylated primer P3 replaces adaptor Ad-2 on the gDNA products.
library_generation.note: P3 (5' biotin) primes off the Ad-2 common region; the biotin tag marks the adaptor-bearing ends for later removal.

library_generation.title: gDNA branch: sonication & streptavidin removal of adaptor ends
library_generation.summary: Products are sheared to ~300 bp and biotinylated adaptor fragments removed on streptavidin beads, keeping internal genomic fragments.
library_generation.note: Sonication (Biorupter) to ~300 bp; Dynabeads MyOne Streptavidin C1 capture biotin-tagged Ad-2/P3 ends, and the non-biotinylated supernatant (pure genomic inserts) is retained.

library_generation.title: gDNA branch: Illumina library preparation (NEBNext Ultra)
library_generation.summary: Sheared genomic fragments are converted into a cell-indexed Illumina library with the NEBNext Ultra DNA Library Prep Kit.
library_generation.note: End-repair, A-tailing, adapter ligation and indexing add P5, a sample index and P7 (standard TruSeq/NEBNext chemistry; exact sequences not printed).

library_generation.title: mRNA branch: second-strand synthesis with P1
library_generation.summary: The cDNA half undergoes a single PCR cycle of mRNA-specific second-strand synthesis with primer P1.
library_generation.note: P1 (5'-CGATTGAGGCCGGTAATAC-3') generates double-stranded cDNA and restores the intact T7 promoter needed for IVT; only cDNA-derived molecules acquire this.

library_generation.title: mRNA branch: in vitro transcription (IVT) to aRNA
library_generation.summary: T7 in vitro transcription linearly amplifies the double-stranded cDNA into antisense aRNA, produced only from cDNA (not gDNA).
library_generation.note: 13 h T7 IVT (MessageAmp II) makes many antisense aRNA copies per cDNA; gDNA lacks a T7 promoter and is not transcribed, enriching the transcriptome fraction.

library_generation.title: mRNA branch: 3' Illumina adaptor ligation to aRNA
library_generation.summary: A 3' Illumina adaptor is ligated onto the aRNA to provide the second priming site.
library_generation.note: CEL-Seq-style ligation (ref 13) of the TruSeq small-RNA 3' adaptor to the aRNA 3' end.

library_generation.title: mRNA branch: RT, PCR & indexed Illumina library
library_generation.summary: The adaptor-ligated aRNA is reverse-transcribed and PCR-amplified into a cell-indexed Illumina mRNA library (CEL-Seq prep).
library_generation.note: RT primes off the ligated 3' adaptor; PCR with indexed Illumina primers adds P5/P7 and a sample index, yielding the sequenceable transcriptome library.

library_sequencing.primer: Illumina Read 1 sequencing primer (TruSeq Read 1 region of Ad-1x; sequence per CEL-Seq ref 13, not printed)
library_sequencing.note: The left mate reads the cell-specific barcode carried by Ad-1x, identifying the cell of origin. The Read 1 primer anneals in the TruSeq Read 1 region and extends rightward through the barcode. 100 bp paired-end on Illumina HiSeq 2500. Internal adaptor/barcode segments are placeholders because their sequences are deferred to CEL-Seq and not printed in the DR-Seq documents; only the P5/P7 flanks are known and shown base-paired.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Illumina Read 1 sequencing primer
                              5'-[TruSeq Read 1]------------------------------------------->  (reads barcode)
5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[TruSeq Read 1 ][CELL_BARCODE ][cDNA insert ]...[i7] ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
3'- TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTCTAGATGTG[TruSeq Read 1'][CELL_BARCODE'][cDNA insert']...[i7']TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'

library_sequencing.primer: Illumina Read 2 sequencing primer (3' TruSeq adaptor ligated to aRNA; sequence per CEL-Seq ref 13, not printed)
library_sequencing.note: The right mate reads the 3' end of the transcript. The Read 2 primer anneals in the TruSeq Read 2 region and extends leftward across the cDNA insert; residual Ad-2 sequence is trimmed computationally and the first mapped coordinate becomes the length-based identifier used to collapse PCR duplicates. 100 bp paired-end.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[TruSeq Read 1 ][CELL_BARCODE ][cDNA insert ][TruSeq Read 2 ]...[i7] ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
3'- TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTCTAGATGTG[TruSeq Read 1'][CELL_BARCODE'][cDNA insert'][TruSeq Read 2']...[i7']TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'
(reads transcript)                      <-------------------------------------[TruSeq Read 2]-5'

title: DR-Seq
description: DR-Seq (gDNA-mRNA sequencing) simultaneously quantifies the genome and transcriptome of the same single cell without physically separating nucleic acids before amplification. A single hand-picked cell is lysed and its mRNA reverse-transcribed with a barcoded poly-T primer (Ad-1x) carrying a 5' Illumina adaptor and T7 promoter; gDNA and single-stranded cDNA are then co-amplified by MALBAC-style quasilinear whole-genome amplification with a random-priming adaptor (Ad-2). The sample is split: one half is PCR-amplified and made into a gDNA Illumina library for copy-number/SNV calling, the other is IVT-amplified (aRNA is produced only from cDNA) and made into a CEL-Seq-style mRNA library. Unique Ad-2 random-priming positions ("length-based identifiers") replace UMIs to remove PCR duplicates and count original cDNA molecules.

reference.label: Single-cell analysis of genome and transcriptome diversity in humans

original_publication.title: Integrated genome and transcriptome sequencing of the same cell

throughput.summary: Low-throughput, plate/tube-based hand-picked single cells; ~70% single-cell amplification success (21/30 SK-BR-3, 13/18 E14). E14: mRNA from 13 cells, gDNA from 3 of them; SK-BR-3: mRNA from 21 cells, gDNA from 7. ~10,674 (E14) and 12,205 (SK-BR-3) genes detected; single-cell gDNA sequenced at 0.6-2.5x depth.
throughput.cells: 13 E14 + 21 SK-BR-3 single cells (integrated gDNA+mRNA from the same cell)
throughput.rna: 3' transcript counting via length-based identifiers; 66/92 ERCC spike-in species detected
throughput.dna: Whole-genome copy-number and SNV calling at 0.6-2.5x single-cell depth

other.label: Quasilinear amplification
other.value: 7 rounds of MALBAC-style quasilinear whole-genome amplification (Bst large fragment + Pyrophage 3173 exo-)

other.label: Amplicon size
other.value: Short 0.5-2.5 kb amplicons; final DNA library ~300 bp

other.label: Sequencer
other.value: Illumina HiSeq 2500; cDNA libraries 100 bp paired-end, gDNA/CEL-Seq libraries 50 or 100 bp paired-end

other.label: Cell lines
other.value: Mouse E14 embryonic stem cells (mm10) and human SK-BR-3 breast cancer cells (hg19)

data_processing.summary: Two computational branches. mRNA: the left mate is demultiplexed by cell barcode, the right mate has the Ad-2 adaptor trimmed and is aligned to the transcriptome with BWA, and the first genomic coordinate of the right mate serves as a 'length-based identifier' to collapse PCR duplicates before RPM transcript counting. gDNA: reads are aligned to a coding-region-masked genome with BWA, PCR-duplicated, variable-binned, coverage-corrected and GC-corrected, segmented by circular binary segmentation (CBS), then calibrated against bulk to call integer copy numbers; SNVs are called with GATK.
data_processing.statistical_model: Circular binary segmentation (CBS) for copy-number breakpoint detection; median segment counts calibrated against a bulk reference to call integer copy numbers.
